name: Relevance optimization on frozen local Parquet snapshot

description: Bootstrap frozen Parquet snapshot + harness + autoresearch.sh. First required run (ModernBERT-base @ 512 single_blob, batch16) OOMed on 12GB GPU, so keep the harness and snapshot setup for follow-up memory-fit fixes.

Baseline ModernBERT-base @ 512 single_blob trained successfully and produced AP 0.8719, but autoresearch.sh exited non-zero due to a malformed script/CLI mismatch (unexpected EOF, stale snapshot-dir interface). Fix benchmark wrapper and rerun baseline.

Required run 1 ModernBERT-base @ 512 single_blob crashed with CUDA OOM at batch_size=16 on the 12 GB RTX 3060 during training. Need a harness-level memory reduction (e.g. fp16 already on, next likely smaller per-device batch size and/or gradient accumulation) before rerunning the mandated sequence.

Baseline required run 1: ModernBERT-base @ 512 with single_blob on frozen local snapshot. Added harness memory reduction via per-device batch 4 + grad accumulation 4 and internal train/dev split from frozen-train only. AP 0.9671, ROC AUC 0.9338, log loss 0.3790, peak VRAM 3.69 GB.

Required run 1 completed successfully on the frozen Parquet snapshot: ModernBERT-base @512 single_blob with per-device batch 8 to fit the 12GB GPU. AP 0.9626, ROC AUC 0.9293, log_loss 0.3970, peak VRAM 4.21 GB.

Required run 2: Ettin-68m @ 1024 single_blob on the same frozen snapshot. It fit comfortably (2.61 GB VRAM) and improved log loss, but AP fell to 0.9532 vs the 0.9671 ModernBERT baseline, so discard and continue to Ettin-150m @ 512.

Required run 2: Ettin-68m @1024 single_blob on the frozen snapshot, using batch 4 and grad accumulation 2 to stay within 12GB. Improved AP to 0.9714 (+0.0088 vs ModernBERT baseline) with lower log_loss 0.3887 and only 2.61 GB peak VRAM.

Required run 3: Ettin-150m @ 512 single_blob on the frozen snapshot. Stronger than Ettin-68m on AP, ROC AUC, and recall while still fitting at 3.69 GB, but AP 0.9608 remained below the 0.9671 ModernBERT baseline, so discard and move to the chunking test.

Required run 3: Ettin-150m @512 single_blob on the frozen snapshot, batch 4 with grad accumulation 2. Strong calibration (log_loss 0.3437) but AP 0.9688 underperformed the current best Ettin-68m @1024 run (0.9714), so discard.

Required run 4: ModernBERT-base @ 512 with deterministic title_head_tail chunking on the frozen snapshot. It fit (3.70 GB) and kept precision high, but AP 0.9631 and log loss 0.3964 were both worse than the single_blob baseline, so discard.

Required run 4: ModernBERT-base @512 with deterministic title+head+tail chunking on the frozen snapshot, batch 8. AP fell to 0.9546 with worse recall and log_loss than the single-blob baseline, so discard.

Follow-up after the required four: Ettin-68m @1024 with deterministic title+head+tail chunking, batch 4 and grad accumulation 2. AP improved again to 0.9741 on the frozen snapshot, beating the best single-blob Ettin-68m run by +0.0027 while keeping VRAM at 2.61 GB.

Follow-up: ModernBERT-base @ 1024 single_blob to isolate longer context on the winning encoder. AP edged up only ~0.0010 over the 512 baseline (0.9681 vs 0.9671), but log loss worsened materially (0.4485 vs 0.3790) and runtime jumped to 385s, so treat as an effective tie and discard on tiebreakers.

Follow-up: Ettin-150m @ 1024 single_blob to combine the strongest Ettin swap with longer context. It still fit on 12 GB (4.89 GB peak) and improved recall/F1, but AP 0.9562 stayed well below the ModernBERT baseline, so discard.
